message:
test(fcp-host): add cancel follow-up and SIGTERM shutdown integration tests

Add two new fcp-host binary integration tests covering cancellation
recovery and graceful shutdown behavior:

cancel_follow_up_invoke_after_cleanup:
- Sends a slow invoke (300ms delay), cancels it mid-flight, then
  immediately sends a follow-up invoke to verify the host correctly
  cleans up cancellation state and can serve new requests
- Asserts cancel response has Cancelled outcome with cleanup_result
- Asserts follow-up invoke succeeds with correct echo output
- Validates structured log event ordering: cancel_response →
  follow-up invoke_request → follow-up invoke_response

sigterm_shutdown_exits_cleanly_and_stops_serving_http (unix only):
- Spawns fcp-host, sends SIGTERM via kill(1), waits for clean exit
- Asserts process exits with success status
- Validates structured log event ordering: sigterm_received →
  shutdown_signal → host_shutdown_complete
- Probes health endpoint post-shutdown to confirm HTTP is no longer
  served (expects connection refused/closed)

Supporting helpers:
- wait_for_host_exit(): polls child.try_wait() with timeout
- send_sigterm(): sends SIGTERM via kill(1) command
- wait_for_log_entry(): waits for a specific structured log entry
  matching an arbitrary predicate

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
